nodes.description: Single-dose intramuscular penicillin treats all stages of pinta.

evidence.snippet: All stages of pinta are treatable with a single intramuscular injection of penicillin.

nodes.description: Treponema carateum maintains its peptidoglycan cell wall by penicillin-binding protein (PBP) transpeptidase cross-linking. This essential, bacteria-specific reaction is the target of the penicillin used to treat pinta: beta-lactam acylation of the PBP active site halts cross-linking and is treponemicidal, which is why a single penicillin injection cures all stages.

evidence.snippet: although glycan chain polymerization occurs in the absence of transpeptidation (for example, in the presence of penicillin), efficient transpeptidation requires ongoing GTase reactions
